title: lauro-cerasus officinalis
intro: Lauro-cerasus officinalis, also known as sweet cherry, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to Asia and Eastern Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its various health benefits. The sweet cherry has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and cough, due to its antiviral and antibacterial properties. Sweet cherry is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections.
